summary: Phase 4 (Premium AI Chat Workspace) tested end-to-end. Backend: 6/7 new conversation tests PASS, 1 CRITICAL FAILURE - AI conversation memory does NOT persist across messages (the assistant cannot recall the startup name even though session_id=conversation_id is passed to LlmChat). 13/13 regression tests PASS (root, signup, auth/me, chat, projects CRUD, dashboard). Frontend: WorkspaceHomePage renders perfectly with all required testids: workspace-home, new-conversation, chat-input, chat-send, chat-attach, chat-mic, ai-orb, open-bi-dashboard, build-my-workspace, bootstrap-progress, bootstrap-success, open-dashboard-cta, 10 quickstart cards, 15 suggested pills, conv-{id} list. Right sidebar shows AI Assistant gradient card + 6-ring Business scorecard (Health 7.4, VC 7.6, Marketing 7.2, Brand 8.4, Pitch 8.4, Overall 7.8) + Quick actions. AI orb opens panel with 6 colored shortcuts + Open BI dashboard. Build-my-workspace flow: extract-startup (200) -> bootstrap (200) -> success card -> /workspace/dashboard. All 8 workspace routes still load.

critical.issue: AI memory is BROKEN. Sending 'My startup is PixelForge AI' then 'What is my startup called?' returns 'you haven't shared your startup's name yet'. Root cause: LlmChat is instantiated fresh per HTTP request in conversations.py:170 - the session_id only provides server-side LLM memory if the integration actually persists session state across processes, but emergentintegrations LlmChat does NOT replay history automatically when a new instance is created. Fix: prepend prior conversation messages (or at least last N) into the prompt, OR send them as repeated UserMessage stream calls before the new one. Two independent runs both failed (deterministic).

action_items: (CRITICAL) Fix AI memory in conversations.py POST /messages: rebuild full conversation history into LlmChat each request. Currently the LLM has zero knowledge of prior turns. Recommended fix: load conv['messages'] from DB and replay them as UserMessage/AssistantMessage before calling stream_message on the new text, OR concatenate prior turns into the system_message context, OR use a persistent LlmChat per session.
(INFO) The chat UI, bootstrap, extract-startup and all sidebar/orb interactions are fully working — only the LLM memory wiring needs to be fixed.
critical_code_review_comments: conversations.py:170 — creating a new LlmChat() per request with the same session_id does NOT magically rehydrate history; the implementation must explicitly pass prior messages. This is the root cause of the memory failure.
conversations.py:191 — auto-title uses `len(conv.get('messages', [])) == 0` correctly (before $push), so first user message becomes the title. Works ✓.
conversations.py:225 — extract-startup correctly enforces 400 on empty transcript ✓ and 404 on unknown conv ✓.
conversations.py:268 — bootstrap inserts project with bootstrapped_from + is_demo=False. Clean. ✓

WorkspaceHomePage.jsx — all required data-testid attributes present (verified via DOM query). Quickstart, suggested pills, conv-{id}, build-my-workspace, bootstrap-progress/success, open-dashboard-cta, ai-orb all correctly wired.

WorkspaceShell sidebar — uses 'nav-ai workspace' (with space) and 'nav-bi dashboard' (with space) instead of dashed variants. Spec allowed either form; both are reachable. Recommend dashed variant for consistency with nav-projects/strategy etc.
Right sidebar Business Scorecard correctly shows '—' for new project with no generations (correct per spec).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all testids, all routes, full bootstrap flow E2E pass)

seed_data_creation: Test run created (and cleaned up) several TEST_ conversations + 1 bootstrapped project (NovaMail-ish), all deleted in test teardown. One real conversation 'I'm building a st...' (PixelForge AI) was also created during the frontend bootstrap E2E and left behind along with a new 'PixelForge AI' project — these are harmless residual seeds for the test user.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 4 frontend is production-ready. Backend regression intact. The ONE remaining bug is AI memory across messages — fix conversations.py send_message to replay db conv['messages'] into the new LlmChat instance before stream_message. After fix, rerun: pytest backend/tests/backend_test.py::TestConversations::test_message_flow_and_memory -o addopts='' . That test sends 3 messages and asserts the third reply contains 'pixelforge'. Use 60-120s timeouts. extract-startup + bootstrap already verified end-to-end via UI (extract 200 in ~10s, bootstrap 200 in ~1s). The build-my-workspace CTA only appears after at least one user message. Right-sidebar 'BI Dashboard' arrow button is UI-only/decorative as noted.
rca of the issue: AI memory bug RCA: send_message endpoint constructs `LlmChat(api_key, session_id=conversation_id, system_message=COFOUNDER_PROMPT).with_model('gemini','gemini-3-flash-preview')` fresh on every POST. session_id alone is not a persistence mechanism on this LLM provider — it's a tagging hint. The actual conversation history that the model sees is just the single UserMessage(text=payload.text) passed to stream_message. Therefore the model has zero prior context. Reproduction: 1) POST /messages 'My startup is PixelForge AI ...', 2) POST /messages 'It's pre-seed ...', 3) POST /messages 'What is my startup called?' -> assistant replies 'you haven't named your startup yet'. Reproduced 2/2 runs. Mitigation: before stream_message, iterate over `conv['messages']` from MongoDB and either (a) call chat.stream_message for each historical message in sequence, OR (b) embed them in the system_message / first UserMessage as transcript context. Option (b) is simpler and cheaper.
